examples.text:
εἴ οἱ καὶ μάλα πολλὸν ἀπόπροθι πίονες ἀγροί […]
eí hoi kaì mála pollòn apóprothi píones agroí[…]
examples.translation: Even though his rich fields lie very far away […]

noun.etymology_text: From Proto-Hellenic *agrós, from Proto-Indo-European *h₂éǵros. Cognates include Mycenaean Greek 𐀀𐀒𐀫 (a-ko-ro), Latin ager, Sanskrit अज्र (ájra) and Old English æcer (English acre). Compare νομός (nomós).
noun.head_info_text:
ᾰ̓γρός • (ăgrós) m (genitive ᾰ̓γροῦ); second declension
(Epic, Attic, Ionic, Doric, Koine)
